name: go
description: Go SDK pipeline adapter for motadatagosdk. Target: Go 1.26 module motadatagosdk.

baselines.scope_note: v0.4.0 ships with files moved to baselines/go/ per Decision D1=B. All consumers (baseline-manager, metrics-collector, learning-engine, G81/G86/G101) updated to read the partitioned paths. The contributes_per_language_partition_to array fills with quality-baselines.json + skill-health-baselines.json IF Decision D2 (shared-core debt-bearer fairness) lands as 'Strict' in v0.5.0; D2 is currently deferred pending Python pilot data. See docs/LANGUAGE-AGNOSTIC-DECISIONS.md.

notes.pass_scope: v0.4.0 scaffolding — structure only. No runtime consumer reads this file yet. Dispatch refactor deferred.
notes.perf_regime_ownership: G104-G110 (perf-confidence regime, rules 32/33) and their owner agents (sdk-perf-architect, sdk-profile-auditor, sdk-complexity-devil, sdk-soak-runner, sdk-benchmark-devil) all live here. They reference Go-specific tooling (pprof, allocs/op from go test -benchmem, goleak). When a second language adapter is authored, equivalent gates + agents go in that adapter.
notes.marker_protocol_ownership: G95-G103 (marker protocol) live here because byte-hash semantics depend on source-byte offsets, which are currently Go-specific. The sdk-marker-protocol skill and sdk-marker-scanner/sdk-marker-hygiene-devil agents stay in shared-core (generalize); the enforcement scripts stay in go (language-specific AST).
